description: CLI pre-call input layer: derive arrayKey from config and accept the scripts' input shorthands so the CLI reaches script-parity on INPUT without changing in-pi ops. Closes FGAP-019 (arrayKey required-but-derivable across 7 block-mutation ops — derive from config.block_kinds[].array_key, demote --arrayKey to optional override by extending cli.ts's required-filter exemption from {writer} to {writer,arrayKey} + an injectArrayKey helper mirroring injectWriter at cli.ts:293-303) and FGAP-025 (--writer kind:id, --where field:op:value, CSV --op in shorthands, each a pre-call transform in parseOpArgs). The ops still receive + require arrayKey; the CLI supplies it.
acceptance_criteria: FGAP-019 closed: append/update/upsert/remove-block-item (+ nested parents) accept --block without --arrayKey; the op is byte-unchanged (still receives + requires arrayKey, the CLI supplies it)
FGAP-025 closed: --writer kind:id, --where field:op:value, and CSV --op in parse to the op's declared param shape before invocation
In-pi op behavior byte-unchanged (frozen-ops constraint honored)
Canonical pipeline: build/check/test + runtime demo (drive the CLI directly) + fresh adversarial audit before green
notes: Release-group: cli-parity (NEXT) | order 1/5 | addresses FGAP-019, FGAP-025 | category: CLI-pre-call (R-0003)
